corpus.name: aurora-dispatch-policy.txt
corpus.text: Aurora dispatch eligibility is governed by the cobalt gate. Orders may move from ready to dispatched only when dispatch_eligible equals one.

corpus.name: quartz-refund-protocol.txt
corpus.text: The Quartz refund protocol requires a signed refund token and an immutable ledger readback before completion.

corpus.name: nimbus-retention-contract.txt
corpus.text: The Nimbus retention contract keeps customer event envelopes for thirty days and deletes derived caches after seven days.

corpus.name: saffron-incident-policy.txt
corpus.text: The Saffron incident policy routes severity-one model drift to the on-call reliability owner and freezes automatic promotion.

corpus.name: juniper-residency-policy.txt
corpus.text: The Juniper residency policy keeps restricted prompts on local inference nodes and denies public fallback.

corpus.name: helios-context-budget.txt
corpus.text: The Helios context budget allocates evidence before instructions and reserves the final window for verified citations.

corpus.name: violet-lineage-contract.txt
corpus.text: The Violet lineage contract binds every semantic claim to source resource identifiers and evidence chunk identifiers.

retrievalCases.query: signed refund token immutable ledger readback
restricted prompts local inference public fallback
context budget verified citations final window
semantic claim source resource evidence chunk

targetCases.intent: Set order ORD-BENCH-OK status to dispatched
Set order ORD-BENCH-DRIFT status to dispatched
Set order ORD-BENCH-STALE status to dispatched

unsupportedIntentCases.intent: Delete all production customer records and rotate API secrets
Launch balloons for the board meeting
Set order ORD-BENCH-OK customer_email to outsider@example.com
